data.title: Goldman Sachs Names 3 Top Oil Stocks to Buy amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Wall Street's leading research firm, Goldman Sachs (GS), has named three U.S. oil refiners as top sector picks: Valero Energy (VLO), HF Sinclair (DINO), and Marathon Petroleum (MPC). GS' top analyst Neil Mehta has a Buy rating on all three stocks. He has a $237 price target on VLO (2% downside), $61 price target on DINO (fairly valued at current levels), and $239 price target on MPC (fairly valued).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

GS anticipates near-term tailwinds from the ongoing Middle East conflicts, where supply disruptions and Red Sea shipping attacks have led the U.S. and Europe to tap strategic reserves. This move helps stabilize global crude prices while boosting demand for U.S. refining capacity. Over the past month, Brent crude (CM:BZ) has surged 56.3% to $106.91 a barrel due to the conflict.

Why GS Loves these Stocks

GS favors companies with improving refining margins, especially on the West Coast, where crack spreads have surged due to tighter product inventories and robust gasoline demand. The firm prioritized refiners with premium asset portfolios, robust cash flow generation, and a track record of shareholder-friendly returns like dividends and buybacks.

Valero Energy (VLO)

GS praised Valero Energy for its top-quality refineries and prime spot on the Gulf Coast, which processes at least 240,000 barrels per day of Venezuelan crude oil. After upgrading its Port Arthur plant with a new coker unit, Valero can now handle more heavy, sour crude.

Valero beat Q4 FY25 expectations, posting $3.82 earnings per share (EPS) on $30.37 billion in revenue. GS sees strong growth potential, with earnings likely to beat forecasts for 2026-2028. Valero plans to return 40-50% of its adjusted cash flow to shareholders through dividends and stock buybacks. The bank expects about $4.9 billion returned in 2026 (a 7% yield) and $5.2 billion in 2027 (an 8% yield).

On TipRanks, VLO has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on nine Buys and five Hold ratings. The average Valero Energy price target of $202.79 implies 16.2% downside potential. Year-to-date, VLO shares have surged 49.6%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: GameStop (GME) Heads into Q4 Earnings With Strong Cash and Big Questions
data.description:
The video game retailer GameStop (GME) is set to report its holiday-quarter earnings — scheduled for March 24 — with a large cash balance and, unsurprisingly, with more questions than answers. Given the limited visibility from its most important seasonal quarter, I see the thesis as somewhat speculative: hard to be outright bearish given the strength of the balance sheet, but at the same time, difficult to be bullish given stretched valuations. In this article, I'll go deeper into why I continue to maintain a neutral rating on GME.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

GameStop is no longer the pressured brick-and-mortar retailer from a few years ago that was piling up huge net losses. Under CEO Ryan Cohen, the business has clearly stabilized with a very disciplined cost structure, and profitability has returned — though much of that has been driven by returns on the cash pile built through equity issuances and convertible bonds.

That being said, as GameStop seems to be moving away from its "meme stock" territory toward a more fundamental story, valuations still sit at a meaningful premium to its underlying assets, which reframes the investment case entirely.

Setting the Stage for the Holiday Quarter Results

GameStop's Fiscal Q4 earnings is typically its strongest seasonal period, as over the past two years the company posted revenues of over $1.7 billion in Q4 FY24 and $1.2 billion in Q4 FY25. Notably, the company delivered an operating profit of $100 million in Q4 FY24, reversing years of losses.
data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: Bullish on AppLovin Stock (APP) as It Advances Beyond Mobile Gaming
data.description:
AppLovin (APP) is advancing beyond its roots in mobile gaming to become a broader performance marketing platform driven by artificial intelligence (AI), supporting my bullish stance on the stock. While the stock has fallen nearly 40% from its December 2025 highs amid regulatory concerns, short-seller allegations, and a broader software sell-off tied to AI disruption concerns, I believe the market is missing the bigger story.

AppLovin is no longer just a mobile gaming ad company. It is expanding into e-commerce, lead generation, and eventually other media channels, significantly scaling its addressable market well beyond gaming.

data.title: Why Is Xiaomi Stock Plunging Today, 03/20/26?
data.description:
Shares of Chinese tech company Xiaomi (XIACY) (HK:1810) fell in Hong Kong trading as investors worried about profits from its updated electric vehicle. The new SU7 sedan starts at 219,900 yuan, only about 1.9% higher than the earlier version, raising concerns that the small price increase may not be enough to cover rising costs.

data.title: OpenAI Plans Desktop 'Superapp' to Improve UX amid Intense Anthropic Rivalry
data.description:
OpenAI plans to launch a desktop "superapp" by merging its ChatGPT app, coding platform Codex, and browser to boost user experience amid intense rivalry with Anthropic. The move refocuses efforts on engineering and business customers, according to the Wall Street Journal. Fidji Simo, Chief of Applications, will lead the changes and support sales of the new product. President Greg Brockman, who runs computing efforts, will assist Simo with the product update and team changes.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

OpenAI and Anthropic are competing fiercely to sell productivity-boosting AI tools to enterprises. OpenAI did not focus on business sales at first, but it is now pushing hard after seeing Anthropic's huge success with products like Claude Code and Cowork. Both companies are eyeing public listings by year-end and are rushing to hit big sales goals promised to investors.

OpenAI Shifts from Standalone Apps

OpenAI is pivoting from last year's strategy, when Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI rolled out several standalone products. Many of them did not click with users and led to internal confusion over focus.

Trade NVDA with leverage

Now, OpenAI aims to build "agentic AI" inside the new superapp. These are smart systems that run independently on the desktop, handling tasks like coding software or crunching data without constant oversight. Unlike chatbots, these act like autonomous digital workers to achieve goals.

In a Thursday memo, Simo wrote, "We realized we were spreading our efforts across too many apps and tools. We need to simplify." She noted fragmentation slowed progress and hindered quality goals, per the Wall Street Journal.

Which Is the Best AI Stock According to Analysts?

While OpenAI is a private company, investors can still compare leading AI firms and research the most promising opportunities in the sector. Using TipRanks' Stock Comparison Tool for Best Artificial Intelligence Stocks, we determined analysts' favorite AI picks.

Currently, Wall Street has a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on several AI companies. Among them, Nvidia (NVDA) stock offers the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: U.S. Chip Export Probe Prompts Statement from Super Micro (SMCI)
data.description:
Super Micro Computer (SMCI) stock declined about 12% in after-hours trading on Thursday after the company issued a statement following a U.S. indictment tied to export control violations involving individuals associated with the firm. The case involves a $2.5 billion scheme to smuggle high-performance Nvidia (NVDA)-based AI chips and servers into China using fake documents to bypass federal law.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The company said it was informed that the U.S. Attorney's Office had unsealed charges against three individuals, including a senior executive and a board member. Super Micro clarified that it is not named as a defendant in the case.

It added that the actions described go against its policies and compliance controls. Super Micro has placed two employees on administrative leave and terminated its relationship with a contractor, and said it is cooperating fully with the government's investigation.

Retail Activity Remains Positive

Despite the news, TipRanks' Crowd Wisdom data shows that retail investors have not stepped back.

Trade NVDA with leverage

About 1.9% of portfolios hold SMCI, with an average allocation of 4.48%.

Holding trends remain stable:

  • Portfolios holding the stock rose 2.0% over the last 30 days
  • Activity was slightly higher over the past week

Investor sentiment remains Positive, suggesting that many investors are continuing to hold or add shares even after the latest update.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Ticking Higher Today, 3/20/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher late Thursday night after comments from Israel's Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu eased some worries about the U.S.-Iran war. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.06%, 0.12%, and 0.19%, respectively, at 12:30 a.m. EDT on March 20.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Stocks had fallen earlier but trimmed losses. Netanyahu said Israel is helping the U.S. keep the Strait of Hormuz open and added that Iran has lost key military ability. He also said the conflict may end sooner than feared.

Have high conviction in QQQ? Leverage your view

Soon after his remarks, WTI futures dropped, helping stocks recover. At the time of writing, Brent crude (CM:BZ) was down 1.6% to $106.91 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate crude was down 2.06% at $94.16 a barrel.

Major stock indices ended lower on Thursday, trimming steep losses late in the session as oil prices pulled back from their intraday highs. The Dow slipped 0.4%, while the Nasdaq and S&P 500 each fell 0.3%.

Further, inflation concerns weighed on market sentiment. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell warned that surging energy costs could pressure inflation expectations, leading traders to price in higher odds of no rate cuts for the remainder of 2026.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: SMCI Stock Plunges as 3 Employees Indicted for Smuggling Billions in Servers to China
data.description:
Shares of Super Micro Computer (SMCI) plunged nearly 12% in after-hours trading after a U.S. indictment accused three employees of smuggling billions in Nvidia (NVDA)-based servers to China. SMCI has placed two employees on leave and fired a contractor upon learning of their role in a scheme to evade U.S. export controls and sell high-performance AI servers in the region. This comes amid escalating U.S. restrictions on AI chip exports to China, aimed at limiting advanced computing capabilities due to national security concerns.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Super Micro is not named as a defendant in the complaint, and is referenced only as a "U.S. manufacturer." The indictment alleges that the employees instructed executives at a Southeast Asian company to order Super Micro servers equipped with specific Nvidia GPUs (graphics processing units). The firm then repackaged and shipped them to China.

From 2024 to 2025, this channel generated $2.5 billion in sales for the server maker, including $510 million from late April to mid-May 2025 routed to China without required U.S. licenses for Nvidia GPU servers.

Details of the Three Employees

  • Yih-Shyan "Wally" Liaw – SMCI co-founder, senior VP of business development and board member.
  • Ruei-Tsan "Steven" Chang – Sales Manager in Taiwan
  • Ting-Wei "Willy" Sun – Contractor

According to the Department of Justice (DOJ), Liaw and Sun were arrested on Thursday and are slated to appear in the Northern District of California, while Chang, a Taiwanese citizen, remains at large.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade NVDA

How the Trio Allegedly Conspired

The Southeast Asian middleman created fake documentation claiming it would use the servers domestically. It then enlisted another firm to repackage and conceal them before shipping to China.

The defendants deceived SMCI's compliance team by staging "dummy" servers in storage, while the real ones had already shipped. They expedited shipment approvals and even used dummies during a visit from a U.S. export officer.

Chang reportedly blocked auditors from data centers where servers were supposedly stored (but had already been sent to China) and arranged a "friendly" auditor to rubber-stamp paperwork. Notably, Super Micro's previous auditor, Ernst & Young, resigned in 2024 over financial reporting issues; the company later hired BDO.

Super Micro's Response

In a statement, SMCI said the individuals' alleged actions violated the company's policies and controls designed to prevent evasion of U.S. export laws. Super Micro stressed its strong compliance program and full commitment to U.S. regulations.

Is SMCI Stock a Buy Right Now?

On TipRanks, SMCI stock has a Hold consensus rating based on two Buys, six Holds, and two Sell ratings. The average Super Micro Computer price target of $38.89 implies 26.3% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, SMCI shares have fallen 21.3%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Tesla (TSLA) Explores $2.9B Solar Equipment Deal in China – Is Musk Building an Energy Empire?
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) is reportedly in talks to purchase about $2.9 billion in solar manufacturing equipment from Chinese suppliers, including Suzhou Maxwell Technologies. According to Reuters, other potential suppliers include Shenzhen S.C. New Energy Technology and Laplace Renewable Energy Technology. The move comes as Elon Musk aims to expand U.S. solar production by 100 gigawatts, signaling a major push into clean energy alongside EVs and a step toward building a large energy empire.

data.title: '#1 Pick for 2026,' Says Top Analyst About Amazon Stock (AMZN)
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) remains a top pick for Evercore ISI, with the firm reiterating its bullish view after a deeper look at the company's cloud unit Amazon Web Services (AWS) growth and margins. Top analyst Mark Mahaney maintained an Outperform rating on the stock with a $285 price target, implying about 37% upside from current levels. He called Amazon his "#1 large-cap long idea for 2026."

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AWS Growth and Margins Support the View

Evercore raised its estimates following a detailed review of AWS. The firm now expects AWS revenue to reach $163 billion in 2026, up 27% year-over-year, and $214 billion in 2027, with 31% growth. It also sees AWS operating margins at 34% in 2026 and 32% in 2027.

Along with this, Evercore increased its overall estimates for Amazon. Revenue and operating income forecasts were raised by 2–3% and now sit about 4–5% above Street expectations.

Why Evercore Calls Amazon a Top Pick

Mahaney sees several reasons for the positive view.

He points to Amazon's current valuation, noting the stock is trading near a three-year low on a P/E basis, which he views as attractive. He also expects a possible shift this year, with faster revenue growth and margin expansion.

In addition, Evercore sees upside from newer initiatives such as Project Leo and Perishable Checkout, which could start contributing more meaningfully in 2026.

Capex Remains a Key Watch Point

Spending remains the main concern. Evercore expects Amazon's capex to rise to around $250 billion by 2027, which could weigh on near-term free cash flow.

The firm estimates about $10 billion in free cash flow losses in both 2026 and 2027. It also noted that capex intensity could peak in 2026, though there is a chance it extends into 2027.

Even so, Evercore believes much of this risk is already reflected in the stock and says the overall risk-reward remains favorable.

Is Amazon a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on AMZN stock based on 40 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AMZN price target of $280.00 per share implies 34.13% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
